article_items.description: Star Wars Zero Company's developers released a gameplay trailer, which features a short appearance by Anakin Skywalker.

article_items.summary: Star Wars Zero Company was announced in 2022 as one of the games being co-developed by Respawn Entertainment, the studio behind Jedi: Fallen Order and Jedi: Survivor . Star Wars Zero Company's developers released a gameplay trailer, which features a short appearance by Anakin Skywalker. The developers behind Star Wars Zero Company has revealed the official gameplay trailer for the title at Summer Game Fest 2026 . You can now preorder Star Wars Zero Company for $70 in Standard Edition for the PC, the PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. They have also revealed that the game will be available on the PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S starting on August 27.

The studio includes former Firaxis Games employees and was also founded by Greg Foertsch, the senior art director on XCOM: Enemy Unknown and XCOM 2 .

"We've worked hand-in-hand with Lucasfilm Games to create an authentic Star Wars story packed with unique new characters, robust character customization, a new ship, Separatist Droids, and much more, all rooted in the.
Respawn developed the game with a new studio called Bit Reactor, with EA as the publisher.
As Hawks, you'll have to recruit allies across the galaxy and across species to put a team together and hunt Kundri Fathom, the leader of a Dark Side cult that poses a threat to the entire galaxy.
The "Den" will be your base of operations, from which you'll form teams and dispatch groups of your friends, called Operators, to different locations in the map.
And because of the game's setting, you'll encounter Anakin Skywalker, a Jedi General at that point in time, who's also on an important mission.

The Musk-owned company also has a contract with Anthropic , giving the latter access to xAI's Colossus 1 data center.

According to a SpaceX filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission, it will receive $920 million a month from Google in exchange for computing power from xAI's data centers, starting in October this year until.
Google has just signed a $30 billion AI deal with SpaceX, which owns Elon Musk's xAI .
The agreement will give Google access to 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, as well as CPUs and memory.
According to the documents SpaceX filed for its Initial Public Offering, Anthropic will pay it $1.25 billion a month through May 2029.
Google isn't its only competitor in the AI space that SpaceX is selling computing power to.
Since Google has its own global network of data centers and continues to build more , you may be wondering why it has to pay SpaceX for more computing power.
SpaceX is expected to go public on June 12 and to raise enough money to make it the biggest IPO in history .

Well, a Google Cloud spokesperson told CNBC and The New York Times that this is but "a short-term, timely agreement" to ensure that the company has "bridge capacity to meeting the surging demand" for Gemini Enterprise.

SpaceX wrote that if it fails to deliver access to that number of GPUS by September this year, Google could either terminate their deal immediately or accept the number of GPUs provided at a reduced payment rate after a.
